channel.title: Last Day - NEW & UPDATE
channel.description: All NEW and UPDATE articles from the last day with enhanced metadata

item.title: NEW: Microsoft Patches 172 Flaws, Including Three Actively Exploited Zero-Days
item.description:
Headline: Microsoft Patches 172 Flaws, Including Three Actively Exploited Zero-Days

Summary:
Microsoft's October 2025 Patch Tuesday update is one of the largest of the year, addressing over 172 vulnerabilities across its product suite. The release is critically important as it includes patches for three zero-day vulnerabilities under active attack. These include an elevation of privilege flaw in the Windows Remote Access Connection Manager (CVE-2025-59230), which has been added to CISA's KEV catalog. Additionally, a highly critical, pre-authentication remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2025-59287) in the Windows Server Update Service (WSUS) with a 9.8 CVSS score requires immediate attention. The update also marks the final security patch for most versions of Windows 10, pushing organizations towards migration.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Patch Management, Vulnerability, Security Operations

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: Microsoft's October 2025 Patch Tuesday addresses over 172 vulnerabilities, including three actively exploited zero-days (CVE-2025-24990, CVE-2025-59230, CVE-2025-47827) and a critical WSUS RCE flaw (CVE-2025-59287).

Article Slug: microsoft-october-2025-patch-tuesday-fixes-172-flaws-and-three-zero-days

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: F5 Hacked by Nation-State Actor; BIG-IP Source Code Stolen
item.description:
Headline: F5 Hacked by Nation-State Actor; BIG-IP Source Code Stolen

Summary:
F5 Networks has disclosed a severe security incident involving a 'highly sophisticated nation-state threat actor' that gained long-term access to its development environment. The attackers, suspected to be the Chinese espionage group UNC5221, successfully stole source code for F5's flagship BIG-IP products. While F5 found no evidence of a software supply chain compromise, the theft of these 'digital blueprints' creates a significant risk of future zero-day vulnerabilities. The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) has issued an urgent advisory, and F5 released a large batch of 44 new vulnerability patches concurrently with the disclosure.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: F5 Networks reveals a sophisticated nation-state actor breached its development environment, stealing source code for BIG-IP products. The attack is linked to the suspected Chinese espionage group UNC5221.

Article Slug: f5-discloses-nation-state-breach-source-code-stolen

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: CISA Warns: Critical Adobe AEM Flaw (CVSS 10.0) Actively Exploited
item.description:
Headline: CISA Warns: Critical Adobe AEM Flaw (CVSS 10.0) Actively Exploited

Summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued an urgent warning for a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) Forms, tracked as CVE-2025-54253. The flaw, which carries a perfect 10.0 CVSS score, allows for unauthenticated arbitrary code execution and is being actively exploited in the wild. CISA has added it to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, mandating federal agencies to patch by November 5, 2025. The vulnerability stems from a misconfiguration in JEE versions of AEM that exposes a debug servlet, allowing attackers to achieve full system compromise.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: CISA warns of a critical, actively exploited remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2025-54253) in Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) Forms with a CVSS score of 10.0. Patch immediately.

Article Slug: cisa-warns-of-actively-exploited-adobe-aem-flaw-with-cvss-10

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Lending Platform Prosper Breached, 17.6 Million Accounts Exposed
item.description:
Headline: Lending Platform Prosper Breached, 17.6 Million Accounts Exposed

Summary:
The peer-to-peer lending platform Prosper has confirmed a massive data breach that exposed the personal and sensitive information of approximately 17.6 million user accounts. The breach notification service 'Have I Been Pwned' has already incorporated the data set, which includes names, email addresses, and phone numbers. The incident places millions of users at a significantly higher risk of targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and other fraudulent activities. Affected users are strongly advised to change their passwords and enable multi-factor authentication immediately.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Regulatory

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: The peer-to-peer lending platform Prosper has confirmed a massive data breach exposing the personal information of approximately 17.6 million user accounts, including names, emails, and phone numbers.

Article Slug: lending-platform-prosper-reports-data-breach-affecting-17-million

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: UK Fines Capita £14M for "Preventable" 2023 Data Breach
item.description:
Headline: UK Fines Capita £14M for "Preventable" 2023 Data Breach

Summary:
The UK's Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) has levied a £14 million fine against outsourcing giant Capita for significant data protection failures related to a March 2023 data breach that impacted 6.6 million people. The ICO's investigation concluded the breach was 'preventable' and heavily criticized Capita's slow incident response, noting that a compromised device remained active on the network for 58 hours after detection, allowing for further exploitation. The penalty highlights the increasing regulatory focus on the speed and efficacy of breach containment.

Severity: Medium

Categories: Regulatory, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: The UK's Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) has fined outsourcing firm Capita £14 million for data protection failures related to a March 2023 data breach, citing preventable errors and slow incident response.

Article Slug: uk-regulator-fines-capita-14-million-for-preventable-2023-data-breach

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: CISA Issues 13 Advisories for Critical ICS/OT Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Headline: CISA Issues 13 Advisories for Critical ICS/OT Vulnerabilities

Summary:
On October 16, 2025, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) released a significant batch of thirteen advisories for vulnerabilities affecting Industrial Control Systems (ICS). These alerts impact widely used Operational Technology (OT) products from major vendors including Rockwell Automation, Siemens, Hitachi Energy, Schneider Electric, and Delta Electronics. The flaws pose a direct risk to critical infrastructure sectors such as manufacturing and energy. CISA is urging all asset owners and operators to review the advisories and implement the recommended mitigations immediately.

Severity: High

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Vulnerability, Patch Management

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: CISA released thirteen advisories on October 16, 2025, detailing vulnerabilities in Industrial Control Systems (ICS) from vendors like Rockwell Automation, Siemens, and Schneider Electric.

Article Slug: cisa-issues-13-advisories-for-industrial-control-systems

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: UK NCSC Reports Record "Nationally Significant" Cyber Attacks, Doubles From Last Year
item.description:
Headline: UK NCSC Reports Record "Nationally Significant" Cyber Attacks, Doubles From Last Year

Summary:
The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) has reported a record-breaking 204 'nationally significant' cyber attacks in its 2025 Annual Review, more than doubling the 89 incidents from the previous year. The report highlights a concerning and widening gap between the scale of cyber threats and the UK's national defenses. The NCSC explicitly named China as a 'highly sophisticated and capable threat actor' and warned of near-term challenges from AI-assisted vulnerability research, emphasizing the shared responsibility of defending the UK's Critical National Infrastructure (CNI).

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Cyberattack

Article Type: Report

Meta Description: The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) 2025 Annual Review reports a record 204 'nationally significant' cyber attacks, more than doubling from the previous year, and identifies China as a primary threat.

Article Slug: uk-ncsc-reports-record-number-of-nationally-significant-cyber-attacks

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Swiss Authorities Warn of AKIRA Ransomware Surge, 200 Companies Hit
item.description:
Headline: Swiss Authorities Warn of AKIRA Ransomware Surge, 200 Companies Hit

Summary:
Swiss federal authorities, including the NCSC and fedpol, have issued a joint warning about a significant escalation in cyber extortion campaigns by the AKIRA ransomware group. The group has reportedly attacked approximately 200 Swiss companies, causing damages in the millions of Swiss francs. The attacks, which have recently intensified to a rate of four to five per week, are primarily exploiting insecure remote access methods, such as VPNs and RDP that are not protected by two-factor authentication. Authorities are urging companies to secure their remote access points and report any incidents.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Cyberattack

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Swiss federal authorities warn of a significant increase in attacks by the AKIRA ransomware group, which has targeted approximately 200 Swiss companies by exploiting insecure VPN and RDP access.

Article Slug: swiss-authorities-warn-of-surge-in-akira-ransomware-attacks

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: California Enacts Stricter Data Breach Law with 30-Day Notification Deadline
item.description:
Headline: California Enacts Stricter Data Breach Law with 30-Day Notification Deadline

Summary:
California has enacted Senate Bill 446, a new law that significantly shortens the data breach notification timeline for businesses. Organizations must now inform affected California residents of a data breach involving unencrypted personal information within 30 calendar days of its discovery. This amendment to the state's already stringent privacy laws places increased pressure on companies to have highly efficient incident detection and response processes in place to meet the accelerated deadline.

Severity: Medium

Categories: Regulatory, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: California enacts Senate Bill 446, a new law requiring businesses to notify California residents of a data breach within 30 calendar days of its discovery, reinforcing the state's strict privacy laws.

Article Slug: california-enacts-stricter-data-breach-law-30-day-notification

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Ransomware Attacks Surge 36% in Q3 2025, Data Stolen in 96% of Cases
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Attacks Surge 36% in Q3 2025, Data Stolen in 96% of Cases

Summary:
A new report from cybersecurity firm BlackFog reveals that publicly disclosed ransomware attacks surged by 36% year-over-year in the third quarter of 2025, setting a new record. The analysis highlights the near-universal adoption of double-extortion tactics, with data exfiltration occurring in 96% of all incidents. The Qilin ransomware group was identified as the most active publicly attributed gang. Healthcare remained the most targeted public sector, while manufacturing was the hardest-hit sector in non-disclosed attacks, underscoring the pervasive and growing threat of ransomware across all industries.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Threat Actor

Article Type: Report

Meta Description: A Q3 2025 report from BlackFog reveals a 36% year-over-year increase in ransomware attacks, with data exfiltration occurring in 96% of incidents. The Qilin group was the most active.

Article Slug: ransomware-attacks-surged-36-percent-in-q3-2025

Published Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Akira Ransomware Gang Actively Exploiting SonicWall VPNs for Network Breaches
item.description:
Headline: Akira Ransomware Gang Actively Exploiting SonicWall VPNs for Network Breaches

Summary:
The Akira ransomware group is actively exploiting vulnerabilities in SonicWall SSL VPN devices to gain initial access to corporate networks. By targeting these widely used, internet-facing appliances, the threat actors can establish a foothold, move laterally, exfiltrate sensitive data, and ultimately deploy the Akira ransomware payload. This campaign underscores the critical importance of promptly patching edge devices and enforcing multi-factor authentication for all remote access solutions to defend against sophisticated ransomware attacks.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Vulnerability

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 6 minutes

Meta Description: The Akira ransomware group is exploiting vulnerabilities in SonicWall SSL VPN devices to breach corporate networks, steal data, and deploy ransomware. Learn how to protect your organization.

Article Slug: akira-ransomware-exploits-sonicwall-ssl-vpn-devices

Published Date: Sat, 11 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 11 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Ransomware Attacks Surge by 46% as Threat Actors Target Construction and Manufacturing
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Attacks Surge by 46% as Threat Actors Target Construction and Manufacturing

Summary:
Despite a slight decrease in overall weekly cyber attacks, ransomware activity has surged by 46%, according to a new report from Check Point Research. This indicates a strategic shift by threat actors towards more focused and impactful ransomware campaigns. The construction, business services, and industrial manufacturing sectors have been the most victimized, bearing the brunt of this new wave. The report identifies the Qilin ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group as one of the most prominent actors, responsible for 14.1% of publicly disclosed victims. The findings highlight an urgent need for organizations, especially in the industrial and business services sectors, to bolster their defenses against an increasingly targeted ransomware threat.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: Report

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: A new report from Check Point Research reveals a 46% surge in ransomware activity, with the construction, business services, and industrial manufacturing sectors being the most targeted by groups like Qilin.

Article Slug: ransomware-attacks-surge-46-percent-construction-manufacturing-hit-hardest

Published Date: Wed, 15 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 15 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: CISA Orders Urgent Patching After Chinese Hackers Steal F5 Source Code
item.description:
Headline: CISA Orders Urgent Patching After Chinese Hackers Steal F5 Source Code

Summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued Emergency Directive 26-01, ordering federal agencies to take immediate action after F5 disclosed a severe breach by a sophisticated nation-state actor, reportedly linked to China. The attackers maintained access for at least a year, exfiltrating proprietary source code for F5 BIG-IP products and details of unpatched vulnerabilities. This breach poses an imminent supply chain risk, as the stolen data could allow adversaries to craft powerful zero-day exploits against F5 customers worldwide, including government and critical infrastructure.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Article Type: Advisory

Reading Time: 6 minutes

Meta Description: CISA has issued an emergency directive after a sophisticated nation-state actor, linked to China, stole F5 BIG-IP source code and details of undisclosed vulnerabilities, creating an imminent supply chain risk.

Article Slug: cisa-issues-emergency-directive-after-nation-state-actor-steals-f5-source-code

Published Date: Thu, 16 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 16 Oct 2025 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 17 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →
